title: brain quarantine resolve <opaqueId> --resolution release|discard [--export-challenge | --authorization <path>] — --json success output
description: PRIVILEGED operator command to resolve a quarantined item (bootstrap-migration.md §7.1). resolution=release re-enters the item into the migrable set on the next graduation migrate; resolution=discard drops it from graduation (recorded, never migrated). Authorized by an OS-mediated presence assertion bound to the broker challenge (op quarantine resolve, mechanism os-presence per security-broker-contract.md §7.4/§7.5) via THREE flows: (1) the interactive OS-presence prompt bound to the challenge in-process (the default), (2) the --export-challenge → sign → --authorization out-of-band flow, or (3) a supplied --authorization; --yes never authorizes. The challenge's intendedEffect binds the quarantine item opaque id + the resolution; the caller identity and the vault are bound by the os-presence mechanism (per-vault broker + trusted-CLI identity), so an authorization cannot cross items/vaults/callers. Each resolve writes an audit record. Idempotent: a repeat resolve with the same idempotency key + request-hash returns the existing outcome. Exit 6 only when presence is unavailable/declined (and no --authorization) or a challenge is exported with --export-challenge (which emits an AuthorizationChallenge instead of this object).

opaqueId.description: Stable opaque id of the quarantined item.

resolution.description: release ⇒ re-enters the migrable set; discard ⇒ dropped from graduation.

outcome.description: Terminal disposition; released ⇔ resolution=release, discarded ⇔ resolution=discard.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Privileged, presence-authorized resolution of a quarantined item (release/discard); audited, idempotent.

flags.description: Required disposition: release (re-migrable) or discard (dropped from graduation).
Emit the broker AuthorizationChallenge (op quarantine resolve, os-presence) to stdout and exit 6 instead of resolving. Omitting it uses the interactive OS-presence flow.

Path to a signed AuthorizationResponse from a prior --export-challenge; presented to the broker to authorize the resolution. Optional when the interactive OS-presence flow satisfies the challenge in-process.
Caller-supplied idempotency key; a repeat resolve with the same key + request-hash (requestHashScope) returns the existing outcome rather than re-resolving (registry idempotency: key-accepting).

sideEffects: writes an audit record of the resolution (allowlisted metadata; never the plaintext)
prohibitedEffects: never authorized by --yes; never bypasses the broker challenge / OS presence assertion
never migrates content itself (release only re-admits it to the next graduation migrate)

errorCodes.when: unknown flag/argument, missing <opaqueId>, missing/invalid --resolution, or --export-challenge combined with --authorization
the interactive OS-presence assertion is unavailable or declined and no --authorization is supplied, or a challenge was exported with --export-challenge
broker: the OS presence assertion is absent or not bound to the challenge
broker drift: the resolution/item differs from the re-derived challenge
broker: the challenge nonce expired (expiresAt passed)
broker: the quarantine item opaque id is not found / not quarantined
broker: the nonce was not issued by this broker
broker: the nonce was already consumed
broker: the quarantine AEAD key is not readable by this trusted-CLI identity
broker: the signerId is not in the signer registry (SP-3: os-presence implemented as a presence-gated signature, §7.1)
broker: a non-presence signer is refused here — only a presence:true p256 signer may hold quarantine ops (§7.1)
broker: the presence-gated signature failed verification (or its prefix disagrees with the enrolled alg)
broker: signingPayload differs from the broker-recomputed canonical bytes
